rule.desc: Using file-level `# noqa` comments to suppress linter warnings across entire modules.
rule.better: Fix underlying issues incrementally. Use targeted inline `# noqa` comments only when genuinely needed.
rule.why_bad: File-level suppressions hide all issues in a file, prevent incremental fixes, and create technical debt.

example.code:
# noqa: ARG002, PLR0913, C901 """Module with many linter violations."""
def process(a, b, c, d, e, f, g): return a + b

"""Module with proper structure."""
def process(values: list[int]) -> int: """Process values and return sum.""" return sum(values)
# Targeted inline suppression when truly needed
def legacy_api_compat(unused_param: int) -> None: # noqa: ARG001 """Legacy API that must maintain signature.""" pass

notes.note: - Inline only: specific line/function needs exception with justification - Temporary: during migration with TODO to remove - Third-party compat: use `# type: ignore` per-line
